evals.prompt: Make me a 10-slide presentation about the rise of AI coding tools. Audience is senior engineers at a mid-size startup. Use the dark developer theme.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file saved to /tmp/ with 10 slides, dark theme (#0f1117 background), keyboard and click navigation, slide counter, and substantive content about AI coding tools. Slides should use varied layouts (not all bullets).
assertions: The output is a valid HTML file (contains <!DOCTYPE html> or <html)
The HTML uses a dark background color — contains #0f1117, #111, #0d0d0d, or a clearly dark background in the CSS
The deck has approximately 10 slides — count elements with class 'slide'; expect 9-11
Keyboard navigation is implemented — JS contains 'ArrowRight' or 'keydown' listener
A slide counter is rendered — HTML contains a counter element showing current/total slide number
Slide content mentions specific AI coding tools by name (e.g. Copilot, Cursor, Codeium, Tabnine, Devin, or similar)
At least two distinct layout types are used — data-layout or class attributes vary across slides (not all identical)

evals.prompt:
Turn this outline into slides:

# Why We're Changing Our Pricing

## The problem
- Enterprise customers outgrew our tiers
- $49/mo cap was limiting deal size
- 3 churned accounts cited pricing complexity

## What's changing
- New plan: $199/mo per workspace
- Unlimited seats (was: $10/seat)
- Annual discount: 20% (was: 10%)

## Impact
- ARR model improves by ~18%
- NPS risk: communicate clearly to existing customers
- Migration: 90-day grace period for legacy plans

## Next steps
- Email to customers goes out Tuesday
- Sales deck updated by EOD Friday
- FAQ page live by next Monday
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML slideshow with slides matching the outline structure. User should be asked only about theme (not content). Slide titles match the headings, bullets are short, layout varies.
assertions: The output is a valid HTML file
The deck has 4-6 slides — each major outline section becomes at least one slide
Pricing specifics from the outline appear in the slides — contains '$199', '$49', 'unlimited seats', or '90-day'
Keyboard navigation is implemented — JS contains 'ArrowRight' or 'keydown' listener
The slides follow the outline structure — titles or sections match The problem, What's changing, Impact, and Next steps

evals.prompt: I want to make a slide deck for a 5-minute lightning talk I'm giving at our company all-hands about why we should adopt TypeScript across all our projects. Clean minimal theme. Keep it tight — 6 slides max.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file with 5-6 slides on TypeScript adoption, clean minimal theme (white background, blue accent), keyboard/click navigation, persuasive content appropriate for an internal all-hands audience.
assertions: The output is a valid HTML file
The deck has 6 or fewer slides — count .slide elements
Clean/light theme is used — background is white, off-white, or light (#fff, #f8f8f8, or similar), NOT dark
Keyboard navigation is implemented — JS contains 'ArrowRight' or 'keydown' listener
Content is specifically about TypeScript adoption — mentions TypeScript benefits such as type safety, IDE support, refactoring, or catching bugs at compile time
